DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SEYFARTH SHAW LLP's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is off calendar as moot in light of the court's ruling on defendant's special motion to strike pursuant to CCP 425.16. Order signed in open court. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-15-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SEYFARTH SHAW LLP's special motion to strike Plaintiff's complaint for legal malpractice under CCP 425.16 is granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted.
Defendant has made a threshold showing that the alleged conduct arises from protected activity under the anti-SLAPP statute. The alleged conduct is written and oral communication by Seyfarth Shaw Attorney, Mr. Newland, with plaintiff's former attorney, Mr. Epstein, regarding a "fraudulent settlement" in connection with the Charles Schwab litigation that commenced in 2008. This oral and written communication is in direct connection with the Charles Schwab litigation, and is protected activity under CCP 425016(e)(2) and the litigation privilege under Civil Code 47. Consequently,the burden shifts to Plaintiff to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on his claim. Plaintiff has not met his burden as he has failed to produce evidence that an attorney-client relationship existed with Mr. Newland and Defendant Seyfarth Shaw, or the Seyfarth Shaw owed a duty to Plaintiff.
Defendant's request for prevailing party attorney fees under CCP 425.16(c) is denied without prejudice. Defendant has not filed a declaration of evidence to support the award of attorney fees. Order signed in open court. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
